title: Making Potassium metal -Magnesium Reduction method.
description:
Making potassium metal.
KEEP IN MIND THIS IS A DANGEROUS PROCEDURE!!!
THINGS CAN GO WRONG!! FIRES CAN RESULT!!!
DO NOT ATTEMPT THIS WITHOUT FIRST KNOWING THE SYNTHESIS AND RISKS INVOLVED!!!

paraffin oil is the medium and high quality 99.9% Mg metal shavings 
and reagent grade KOH and Tert butanol were also used. 
The oil,Mg, and KOH were placed into a 100ml flask fitted with a reflux condenser and heated to 250C. the catalyst was then added and the whole thing left to reflux for several hours yielding K metal by the end of the reaction. 
Lots of credit to the guys at SM: http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=14970
And verschemie for their help with the process used here....

title: A Cold Acetone Distliiation
description:
By using the same setup as shown by Nurdrage here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tYLlkTDstmo
I get acetone to distill at a mere 18C. 
the outside of the boiling flask was cold- cold enough to be covered in condensate.
in contrast my lab temp was nearly 30C!
 If i used ice water in the reservoir it would distill at a lower temperature still! 
 This little aspirator worked far better than i had expected it to. Not bad for a vacuum setup that cost under 50$ to make.
If you dont want to spend $100s + on a vacuum pump this is a very good alternative that works quite well.  adding a manometer would be an improvement which i am lookin

title: Mercury from cinnabar
description:
using a wet method to extract mercury metal from cinnabar- its raw material. we used a solution of sodium hydroxide and elemental sulfur  adding the cinnabar when it had all dissolved and then adding aluminium metal to precipitate the mercury metal.
this  and separated from the crud and was washed cleaning it up to shiny metal. the yield was nearly 38g of clean  product.

title: making calcium cyanamide
description:
Making calcium cyanamide via pyrolysis of calcium cyanurate at high temperature. firstly we made calcium cyanurate using pool stabilizer cyanuric acid in a large solution nad added sodium hydroxide to form sodium cyanurate and finally used  a slight excess of calcium chloride to form the targeted product with sodium chloride as a side product. the calcium cyanurate is somewhat soluble in hot water but further precipitates on cooling. 
We washed and decanted several times to remove all traces of alkali metal contamination- This step MUST be done otherwise dangerous sodium cyanide may form during the heating which will be bad news when the cyanamide is used later in an acidic solution to make Aminoguanidine bicarbonate in a later preparation.

The identity test was nitric acid/cyanamide in a

title: Sodium Azide Recovery from Airbags
description:
WARNING! This process deals with deadly Sodium Azide and the opening of a dangerous article. DO NOT COPY THIS VIDEO!
I am not responsible for any injuries, deaths, property damage, or other issues associated with copying acts depicted in this video. YOU COPY AT OWN RISK!!

In this video i very carefully and slowly disassemble two different Gas Generator units commonly found in older cars (Pre 2001) from both Ford and Holden GM, to recover the gas generation  pellets that fill the airbag in the event of accidents, and separate  NaN3 from the silicon dioxide that is used in their manufacture.
 There was also potassium nitrate used in these pellets, however since both NaN3 and KNO3 are both very water soluble and soluble in common solvents i could not find a good way to *easily*  separate the

title: Making Cesium metal 01
description:
first attempt at making cesium metal by reducing cesium chloride with lithium metal to form the cesium which is distilled under argon using a steel retort with a copper condenser.  not bad for a first try though with nearly a gram of Cs metal made. can finally cross that off my element collection list.   Next is bromine.

 there are a few improvements for next time. i want to make an all steel device, perhaps evacuate the retort before flushing with the argon, a thinner walled retort- this one took forever to heat up and i needed two torches. a better prepared ampoule so it seals properly the first time.

title: Making Hydrazine sulfate
description:
CAUTION! Hydrazine is toxic and corrosive! 
 care must be taken during it's synthesis to not inhale its fumes or come into direct contact with it. it is much safer to handle as it's sulfate salt.  
  
Making Hydrazine sulfate. I used the process published by Magpie from sciencemadness- see here:
http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=15091
I scaled it up though by 2-1/4 times but could have gone even bigger. i had expected the foaming to be much more voluminous like i had experienced in another synthesis. i would have preferred to go bigger and make more at once rather than a few smaller tries.
The resultant yield was a little smaller than i had anticipated(100-110g) but since it is so cheap to produce compared to buying it..... well it doesn't really matter.


Music: Jellyf

title: Making Benzene
description:
In this video i make benzene by reacting sodium benzoate with sodium hydroxide and heat to free the benzene. we distilled it off and collected the rather impure orange distillate.
I had to make a special retort for this purpose using a bit of broken glassware and a 500mls paint can.  using a glass flask would have been better but the fact that glass is attacked by hot NaOH and  the shock when cooling the mass in the flask would crack it if it even lasted that long... so steel it is.

We washed this crude distillate with a couple of portions of distilled water and dried it with calcium chloride.  Next we distilled the crude but washed benzene and collected 96mls of clear purified benzene.

We also did a burn test...

title: Copper Nitro Salt of 5-Aminotetrazole
description:
Since this stuff and most of it's precursors are out of the reach of ordinary non-chemist citizens i thought i would show how this highly energetic compound is made and some testing of it... Enjoy!
We Will see how long this video lasts........

title: Preparation of Isopropyl Nitrite
description:
Isopropyl nitrite (C3H7NO2)
 is a transparent yellow oily solvent which is commonly used in ""poppers" alkyl nitrite preparations which are usually inhaled for the purpose of heightened sexual arousal and desire.  This synthesis is a common preparation and i just wanted to show my take on this synthesis. this synthesis makes a highly flammable solvent whose vapours can induce low blood pressure and dizziness along with potentially deadly NOxx fumes which can cause pulmonary edema with extended exposure. it makes a very strong solvent capable of dissolving chemically resistant vacuum grease and can pass through some gloves. 

 I Plan to use this in a video on making NaN3 in the near future.

inhalation is not advised with this quality of product, and not why i made this video! if you want "

title: From Borax to Elemental Boron
description:
In this video i made Elemental Boron from borax.
Firstly we made boric acid by adding the correct molar amount of HCl to a solution of borax and we recrystallized the resulting boric acid crystals. 
Next we transformed the boric acid into boric oxide using strong heating. we took the glassy brittle solid and milled it into a fine powder. 
This powder was mixed in stochiometric amounts with fine magnesium powder and ignited in a steel vessel to yield our elemental Boron along with some side products. 
 side reaction products and un reacted magnesium are removed by digestion of the whole reaction product in HCl overnight.
We then gravity filtered the boron and washed it on the filter with some dillute potassium hydroxide solution. 
 Finally the filtered boron was put into another 1l beaker w

title: Potassium Picrate
description:
Messing with the mildly explosive and flammable Potassium salt of Picric acid. It,like many salts of picric acid is also an incredibly potent yellow dye. And like many other flammable potassium salts it burns with a lilac coloured flame.

 Once again this is more of a curiosity than a practical and useful compound but it did find use in old pyrotechnic whistling rocket compositions. it does make quite an impressive sound when put into tubes for this purpose.

A Re-Upload from my old "Energy Unleashed" channel which was deleted in july last year.
Just shows a process to construct a copper cone shape with electricity, copper metal and an  acidic copper sulfate electrolyte. this process is similar to copper plating except the layer of metal is deposited many times thicker.

title: Synthesis of Potassium Azide -KN3
description:
In this video i synthesized a small amount of Potassium Azide from 
hydrazine sulfate which had been treated with sodium hydroxide to free the hydrazine. this was put in the reactor and further basified with potassium azide which when isopropyl nitrite is added will form potassium azide.
This synthesis is quite dangerous owing to hydrazoic acid vapour which if inhaled can kill. even in tiny amounts. Do not copy this preparation without first researching it properly and make sure that you have the correct glassware... This CANNOT be done in open beakers or flasks!

title: preparation of 5-Aminotetrazole
description:
This preparation is a variation of Thiele's method to produce 5-Aminotetrazole.
 the production of 5-ATZ was achieved by the reaction of the aminoguanidine salt with a diazotization agent in this case NaNO2/HCl to form the corresponding guanylazide salt, which is then cyclized to 5-ATZ with the use of a base. I used  Ammonia but sodium carbonate will also work .  high temperature is applied  to induce refluxing of the solution.  before cooling the solution the PH is adjusted,  to provide the optimal conditions to precipitate the 5-ATZ.
We recrystallized this from hot water to yield a pure crop of yellowish coloured 5-ATZ crystals.

description:
Quick video of making nitric acid via simple distillation from
potassium nitrate and sulfuric acid. goes off without a problem except minor decomposition from to high heating.

I do want to re-distill with extra sulfuric under vacuum to get 99-100% pure acid or at least as far as it can be taken that way.
I also want to do another cleaning video in which the acid is scrubbed with dry air under mild heating but we will see.

title: Sodium 5-Nitrotetrazolate
description:
forming some sodium 5-NTZ. 
This is a pretty sensitive compound and must be treated with the respect it deserves! When dry this stuff is  highly sensitive to impact flame and friction. Small amounts can cause great damage. 
 it's synthesis is fairly straight forward involving PH changes to induce the formation of copper hydroxide and with heating to decompose this into copper oxide which is filtered off through celite. the resulting filtrate is acidified with HNO3 and is evaporated down to induce crystallization. the NaNTZ requires several re-crystallization steps from both water and acetone to yield a product pure enough for further synthesis into other 5-Nitrotetrazolate metal salts eg- silver, lead, cobalt/perchlorate complexes and the incredibly powerful mercury salt which is one of th

title: Preparation of Picric Acid (Revisited and Revised)
description:
Making picric acid via a nitration of Acetylsalicyllic acid or more commonly known as asprin. We use Ethanol to extract the ASA which is separated via filtration through celite. the filtrate is  evaporated to give us the starting product.
The reaction must be done in a well ventilated  area and in a tall form  beaker. overflows will result otherwise.
Next we sulfonate the ASA in hot concentrated sulfuric acid for an hour and upon slight cooling we begin to add the correct amount of sodium nitrate. this nitrates the ASA firstly into the mono and dinitro forms of ASA and upon the last of the nitrate additions and heating we get the third nitro group attached giving us the trinitrophenol product.
The crude product is then recrystallized from boiling water.
The yield from this  was 47.2g from

title: Neon Light Demo
description:
Just a quick demonstration of a neon light that i constructed.
Out of curiosity i wondered if i could make a neon light in my lab with some things i have around and was quite pleased that i got it to work.

I took an ampoule of 99.9% Neon gas and hooked up a high voltage capacitor to it - this only runs off of a single 18650 Li battery.  i attached the leads crudely with electrical tape and touch the wires giving the characteristic red/orange glow of a neon light in action.
 i just wish my camera focused better in the dark.

title: lightning strikes in slow motion.
description:
some shots of lightning filmed at 1000f/ps.
it looks pretty neat.

title: cleaning up yellow Nitric acid
description:
Cleaning up the nitric acid made in a previous video to get crystal clear WFNA.
to do this we simply use an aquarium bubbler to blow dry air through the warmed acid. The air was dried by passing it through calcium chloride. 
 you can do this without stirring but i chose to stir it for better dispersal of the bubbles. 
after a few hours we are left with crystal clear acid which is very suitable for applications where clear acid is a must.

title: Microcrystalline Cellulose Nitrate
description:
in this video i prepare and test some highly nitrated Microcrystalline cellulose. i use a rather uncommon method  in which phosphorus Pentoxide serves as the dehydrating agent rather than sulfuric acid. 
I must say the resulting material is pretty good. it burns quite fast and as i demonstrate it can even  be detonated with the aid of a primer to shock it.
 it is also very stable with the regular NC  i demonstrate  was made in early july last year is just as good as the day it was first made. It was also made using the exact same method i show here.

The method i used can be found in a PDF:
Studies of the nitration of cellulose - Application of new membrane materials.  by Clement cheung 2011.
it is his thesis and it is quite an  in depth study of the study of nitrocellulose made by regular

title: Trichloromethane- Chloroform
description:
In this video i make some chloroform. 
This incredibly useful solvent is created in a haloform reaction using sodium hypochlorite solution and a ketone like M.E.K or acetone whilst keeping the hypochlorite in excess to avoid a difficult to separate  chloroform/acetone azeotrope later on:
3 NaClO + (CH3)2CO → CHCl3 + 2 NaOH + NaOCOCH3
This reaction scheme  is OK for a small scale like this but rather inefficient for industry or a large scale. 
there is a long standing myth surrounding chloroform in the way of knocking people out with a chloroform soaked rag. this almost certainly will not happen unless you hold the rag for at least 5mins!
However i can tell you that you do get a certain light headedness from exposure to vapous which i found out some years ago having distilled it indoors wit

title: Experiment with Cesium picrate - C6H2CsN3O7
description:
Out of a curiosity i decided to make some Cs Picrate to see how it performed under a flame test. the following was an experiment basically identical process to forming  other metal picrates:

firstly i took a boiling hot solution of about a gram  of Picric acid dissolved in about 35mls of boiling distilled H2O and whilst still hot
i neutralized the acid with some CsOH, also in an aqueous solution. 
as soon as the neutralization had occurred and the solution had cooled a little there was a fairly quick crashing  of crystals from the solution.  i was surprised that as much product formed as it did. i actually thought that it would be more soluble than it was. 
anyway it deflagrated under flame fairly quickly after first turning red and melting then finally flashing off in a nice violet color

title: Making Barium and Strontium Nitrates
description:
In this video i prepared some nitrates of barium and strontium both from their carbonates and Nitric acid as follows:
BaCO3 + 2HNO3 ----) Ba(NO3)2 + CO2 + H2O and residual H2S.
SrCO3 + 2HNO3------) Sr(NO3)2 +CO2 +H2O. 

The reactions were a straight forward acid/base  neutralization
in which i neutralized Nitric acid with each carbonate and evaporated the solutions to form each nitrate in a crystalline form. 
the flame colour testing was a little let down though with the camera not picking up the test very well.

Found out that as well as sodium becoming transparent on a wet filter paper potassium exhibits this strange property also.

 Also annoyed my neighbors with a large chunk of Na in a water filled beaker.

title: Nitroguanidine from guanidine nitrate
description:
we dehydrate the previously made guanidine nitrate in concentrated sulfuric acid to form nitroguanidine. this is done at a temperature of around 10C and below. 
the crude product was recrystallized from boiling water to yield the fluffy needle like crystals of nitroguanidine ready for the next step.
we plan to reduce the nitroguanidine to aminoguanidine for yet another different method to synthesize 5-Aminotetrazole.

title: Aminoguanidine Bicarbonate -Method 1-  CaCN2 + H6N2SO4
description:
Method-1 for making Aminoguanidine bicarbonate from calcium cyanamide and hydrazine sulfate.
I have a second method using nitroguanidine as the starting material  but need to edit the video.....
 we formed the aminoguanidine in acceptable yield by mixing the two dry powders and adding them to water while keeping a very good eye on the PH at all times. the PH is modified with additions of 50% sulfuric acid to keep it at the prescribed 9-9.5. this is reacted for a short while and once again the PH is modified to 7. we then heated the reaction for an hour and filtered off the calcium sulfate by product leaving the product in the filtrate which was slightly affected by oxygen in the air.
the filtrate was again modified by acid addition and sodium bicarbonate was added to precipitate the target

distilling cheap 37% rum to separate the alcohol and further concentrate it by vacuum distillation over CaO.

title: Cleaning the white Phosphorus.
description:
All the white Phosphorus i have made so far was cleaned using  similar methods to what is used in industrial process-  using dilute dichromate in acidic solutions.
 i made a small solution of potassium dichromate with sulfuric acid  and added some of this to the molten P4 while stirring the solution. The amount of dichromate solution and time it was immersed was quite arbitrary and i added as much as i thought i would need- a few mls and kept the cleaning going until i though it looked clean enough.  
eventually it turned out much cleaner than i started, being a nice pale yellow colour rather than the red i started with.
Next i may do a video on converting some of this to red phosphorus.
maybe. it is becoming wintery here.

title: Basic lead picrate
description:
Making and testing of some basic lead picrate.
This is made by very slow introduction of a sodium picrate solution into a very hot and well stirred solution of lead nitrate.
Eventually you are left with relatively insoluble precipitate of lead picrate in residual lead nitrate solution. 
It really should be a darker rusty brown than this is. 
i think it was due to the additions being too fast.
there was hints of it in the end product but it really should be darker. Anyways it did seem to perform satisfactory though, with high flame sensitivity and moderate impact sensitivity. it will be good use as a first fire igniter in detonating devices.
out of all the picrate salts, this is probably the best performing of them all(except the Azo-Clathrate).

title: Calcium cyanamide- Urea/Calcium Oxide method
description:
making calcium cyanamide using a well established procedure in which urea is first melted with calcium oxide to form an intermediate product- calcium cyanate. this is then further heated to around 800C for about an hour which yields the final product- calcium cyanamide.
in all honesty i found this method superior over the wet chemical method i used in another video despite all the ammonia evolved. there was less steps and in a couple of hours you could have a batch of cyanamide ready to go. it also helped greatly that i had excellent temperature control with a furnace i had recently constructed.
in the end the product identity was confirmed using the known method involving ammoniacal silver nitrate which gives us bright yellow silver cyanamide.
See more on Bitchute: 
https://www.bitchute.c

title: Aminoguanidine bicarbonate -Zinc reduction method
description:
making aminoguanidine bicarbonate, this time by the reduction of Nitroguanidine with zinc dust in acetic acid. 
All up i was not really impressed by this method. for all the steps involved making the precursor Nitroguanidine and the time it took for that plus the time to add all the paste plus the waiting for the reaction to complete and finally the difficulty filtering it the yield was poor- just on 21g of end product. quite a few hours went int this for little return. the upside is the precursors are all pretty cheap- but so are the ones used in the cyanamide method.
Testing for completion of reaction with Mohrs salt may have helped indicate complete reaction or not and maybe adding more ice to thin the mixture further. possibly this may have helped yield but above all the filtering need

description:
Doing a classic preparation that i have wanted to do for ages: make prussian blue. i mix  solutions of potassium ferrocyanide and ferric chloride and potassium cyanide /ferric chloride to form the dark blue compound known as prussian blue.

title: Another copper salt of 5-NTZ
description:
DBX-1 is a rather modern primary compound of moderate power derived from 5-aminotetrazole as a starting material. this is refined further into sodium 5-NTZ and reacted with CuCl in a reflux setup to yield the final product. There is a great deal of R&D going into this compound and it is looking promising as a replacement for lead azide in millitary munitions. this makes it quite a significant compound. as with many other compounds made from 5-NTZ it is a sensitive explosive which deserves respect.

title: Synthesis of  1-4 Dioxane
description:
Synthesis of 1-Dioxane. 
Dioxane is a useful solvent mostly used in organic chemistry. it does not see a lot of use these days owing to its carcinogenic nature like many things... it is easily prepared by dehydration of ethylene glycol and the purification of the resulting distillate. it is highly flammable and  has a fruity ethereal odour which i can imagine becoming very sickly if you are exposed for too long. i plan to use this specifically for making metallic sodium and potassium in a similar fashion to NurdRage.

doing a distillation of sulfuric acid based drain opener.
I use my furnace to heat the flask and an air condenser. 
It went surprisingly smoothly with almost no bumping and a decent drip rate. the density of the end product correlates to the azeotropic 98% H2SO4.

title: potassium metal distillation attempt
description:
using calcium metal and potassium chloride i try to make potassium metal like this:  2KCl + Ca = 2K + CaCl2 and a lot of heat.
it did work but not as well as i hoped. i have plans to try this again in the near future but under vacuum. this will lower the temperature required for an easier distillation to proceed.

title: Distillation of Potassium 2 - Under Vacuum.
description:
made a second, more refined attempt at making potassium metal. this time i distilled it under vacuum from KCl and Ca metal. it is exactly the same process as my last attempt but with a much mor improved setup. i ended up with just under 3g of nice clean potassium metal. 
i plan on improving the retort design and scaling up this process.
And also to distill cesium metal with this same setup in the near future. i have quite a bit of Ca leftover and about 35g CsCl. enough for an experiment or two.

See more on Bitchute: 
https://www.bitchute.com/profile/4uBPC9RCH6e9/
And minds: https://www.minds.com/EnergyUnleashed

title: Ammonium Nitrate preparation II
description:
using calcium nitrate and ammonium sulfate to make ammonium nitrate and calcium sulfate. this method is only really viable if you have a vacuum filtration system and methanol otherwise you will have a lot of trouble separating the nitrate from the sulfate cleanly.
See more on Bitchute:

title: How to make a Bunsen Burner portable
description:
Bunsen burners are a brilliant tool for chemists and other scientific applications however they usually are limited by the need to be connected to a gas line or bulky gas bottles. in this quick video i show how to overcome this problem by creating a cheap, portable and easy solution to these issues.
See more on Bitchute: 
https://www.bitchute.com/profile/4uBPC9RCH6e9/
And minds: https://www.minds.com/EnergyUnleashed

title: Chlorine in the Tap Water
description:
testing my drinking water for chlorine with silver nitrate. nothing special....
 Upon getting a drink from the tap this afternoon i could smell just how heavily the water is chlorinated here. It must be dosing day or something. It was nearly as bad as swimming pool water.
 I took a little AgNO3 to the water and was greeted with quite a milky solution. in fact it could not be seen through. 
I have done this test before and although not quantifying anything it shows the high level of  AgCl precipitate in the water. other times i have done this it was still reasonably clear.

I know there's not much to this video but it highlights the crap we are forced to consume- Sadly though there are not many other options.....

title: Preparation of Sodium Methoxide -CH₃ONa
description:
By adding sodium metal to Methanol we get a compound called sodium methoxide-CH₃ONa. 
 it is a useful catalyst and strong reducing agent which sees use in a variety of industries from pharmaceutical to fuel manufacture. i plan to use it as a catalyst for the transesterfication  of castor oil in a future video.
See more on Bitchute: 
https://www.bitchute.com/profile/4uBPC9RCH6e9/

title: Highly Energetic Mercury Compound
description:
making and testing some incredibly energetic 
Mercury 5-Nitrotetrazole.
Out of all the tetrazole based energetics i would have to say that this is the most impressive so far. this stuff is crazy powerful- more so than DBX-1. the confined heat test really was much more powerful than the footage can convey. it really took me by surprise.
out of the three Ag,Cu & Hg this is the best. the downsides are its toxicity and being mercury based can not be used in aluminium detonator tubes without an inert coating.

title: Preparation of Cesium Metal 02
description:
a second and much improved process to make cesium metal from cesium chloride and calcium metal under vacuum.
for this I re used a copper condenser i made for making potassium metal and a retort constructed from steel tube and plate welded together with a 20mm threaded steel pipe to fit the condenser. this was assembled along with a side arm test tube to collect the metal.

After loading the retort the whole setup was evacuated 3 times with a flush of argon in between to exclude all oxygen. 
Heating was commenced and shortly after cesium began to distill over. 
This was transferred to a smaller tube after i was dissatisfied with the ampouling i did at first. the metal was then to be stored under argon until it's use. i have something in store for this.
See more on Bitchute: 
https://www.bit

title: PTFE and Metal powders reacting - IR Decoy Flare compositions
description:
Mixtures of PTFE (teflon) and some metal powders are quite flammable, hot burning mixtures which still see use in many decoy flares which are used to attract heatseeking missiles away from aircraft. they burn hot and emit infrared wavelength light which provides the decoy effect. the mixes here are all 60%PTFE and 40% metals. the powders tested here were: Eckhart Dark Aluminium, 400mesh bright Aluminium, 500mesh Magnesium, 
Manganese, Boron, Zinc and Titanium.
 I did also try unsuccessfully Cobalt ,Nickel,Copper, Tin, Molybdenym and tungsten powders.  a little bit of sparkler was used on some of the mixes to get a good ignition. most of the remaining

title: Making Perchloric Acid -HClO4
description:
Small scale preparation of Perchloric acid. we also test its oxidizer properties with some fuels. 
Firstly we take Ammonium Perchlorate and convert it into Sodium Perchlorate, we did this by adding sodium hydroxide and heating to drive off a bunch of ammonia. 
Next we took the now basic solution and added some Hydrochloric acid in excess.
 the sodium chloride precipitates immediately and we filter it off.
And So begins the fun part......
Boiling off the excess Hydrochloric Acid.
A major portion of the solution boils off at around 112-115c.
once we got it to 150+ we can be assured that the HCl has been boiled off fully.
some precipitate also appeared, likely sodium chloride or sodium Perchlorate. unfortunately i discarded it.....
after leaving the bottle open overnight the yellow colour dis
safety: perchloric acid extreme oxidizer, explosion hazard

title: Making Graphite Substrate Lead Dioxide Anodes
description:
Creating these anodes was not too hard once it was all worked out correctly. after a few failures i found the key seems to be the Pre treatment. getting a nice erosion layer is the difference between having the plating flake off or hold tight. the other parameters are important too but having a good substrate is key.
I am still yet to test them in the very harsh environment of an electrolytic  perchlorate cell so hopefully they last OK. i don't see why not though. 
Other people seem to have had mixed success making decent anodes this way  but i'm confident that these will be up to the task. The plating is quite hard, solid and very well adhering to the graphite. If they only last a short while that is fine too. the starting materials are cheap and it's not hard to do.
I also came across so

title: Running an Ammonia Generator.
description:
Quick video on how i make ammonia solution.
Basically i just used an arbitrary amount of Ammonium Sulfate in the main generator flask and a strong solution of Sodium Hydroxide was dripped onto it forming ammonia gas which was led into a weaker solution of ammonia that i wanted to boost the concentration of.

title: Preparation of Acetamide (2 ways)
description:
I use two different method to make acetamide for an upcoming project - Acetic Anhydride.
I use the Ammonia/Ethyl Acetate reaction.
And the Urea/Glacial Acetic Acid reaction.
In both cases the resulting product was distilled and turned out to be fairly pure with a melting point of 80.7c
After all the methods were completed all the product was collected and distilled together to give me enough product for the next step.
I will be making Acetonitrile from this by dehydrating the Acetamide with P2O5. rom there i will be doing similar to Chemplayer by gassing the Acetonitrile with HCl in the presence of GAA to get Acetyl chloride and finally using this to distill Acetic Anhydride from Sodium Acetate. 
See more on Bitchute: 
https://www.bitchute.com/profile/4uBPC9RCH6e9/

description:

Preparation of A Cobalt complex - Part 1.
 Carbonatotetraamine Cobalt III Sulfate.
Just watch and see how i did it.
Part 2 will be out in a day or two so stay tuned!

title: An Interesting Cobalt Complex - Part II
description:
Following on from part one we continue the synthesis of BNCP.
This stuff is cutting edge, powerful and somewhat insensitive making it an ideal candidate for modern, laser fired munitions and High voltage discharge activations. There are a few more steps involved to complete the synthesis but nothing too overly difficult.
provided you are in possession the correct reagents.
Watch and enjoy!!!

Here are some details of this remarkable compound:
***Tetraamino-Cis-Bis-(5-Nitrotetrazolato-N2)Cobalt III Perchlorate***
- Density: 2.05g/cm3
-Thermally stable up to 200C with flashpoint of 269C.
-Heat of explosion:      3.32Mj/Kg
Detonation velocity:  @1.97g/Cm3----- 8100m/s (Calculated)
-DDT transition: 10-11 microseconds.
-Minimum initiating amount (RDX) 5mg. this is very little!
-Non Toxic, Non H

title: Making 98% Sulfuric Acid by Distillation

title: Restoring Lab Oven via Zinc Plating
description:
Please Support me on Patreon  https://www.patreon.com/aussiechemist

How to get Zinc case from Zinc Carbon Batteries. https://youtu.be/IGKVmnmyNLY

Sacrificial Anodes  are more active metals that are used to prevent a less active material surface from corroding. Sacrificial Anodes are created by plating or attach a metal with a more negative electrochemical potential ( such as zinc )than the metal it will be used to protect. The sacrificial anode will be consumed in place of the metal it is protecting, which is why it is referred to as a "sacrificial" anode.
Brush electroplating technique I used here has several advantages over tank plating, including portability, ability to plate items that for some reason cannot be

title: Make Copper Acetate at Home!
description:
In this video we will show you how to make Copper Acetate starting with Copper Sulfate commonly found as a root killer. Copper Sulfate and Copper Acetate are poisonous if not handled correctly, respect the chemicals. Acetic Acid is corrosive, ensure that proper safety is taken with any acid. Remember wear gloves, and goggles. This is a fun experiment to perform with adults, and children alike. It shows the concept of using solubility to purify products, as well as a fun color change! For a deeper examination of the chemistry I would suggest visiting http://chem.pieceofscience.com/?p=94

For other neat experiments check out some of my other videos!

Hi everyone,
In the previous video I showed you how to make Copper Acetate, using Copper Sulfate as a precursor. Today, I will show you how to make Copper Acetate from Elemental Copper, in our case pennies. To do this experiment we will need two glass containers, distilled white vinegar, and 3% Hydrogen peroxide solution. In addition I recommend getting a notepad, and one scale. While we prepare this experiment a brief history lesson is needed. Modern pennies are made of mostly Zinc, with only about 2.5% copper to coat the outside. However, they weren’t always like this, prior to 1983 they were made of 5% Copper. So in our experiment we will prove this, while also synthesizing Co

title: How to make Prussian Blue!
description:
I hope you all enjoy the video, be sure to comment below!

A rough outline of the first half of the video:

Today we will be making yet another beautiful blue chemical, with great historical footholds. We will be making Iron hexacyanoferrate, also known as Prussian blue. 


Prussian Blue is best known for its deep blue hue, and is commonly used in many forms of art. Most know of Van Gogh’s Starry Night, or The Big Wave, both utilize Prussian Blue. The name Blueprint originally derives from the hue of the print, an artifact of the print process used, called cyanotype, which involves Prussian Blue. 


Enough of the History, onto the synthesis:

For this experiment we will need Iron (II) Sulfate, Potassium Ferricyanide, Glass Beakers, A Glass WatchGlass, A Scale, paper filters, gloves, and go

title: Beautiful Red Copper Complex (Chevreul's salt)
description:
Hi everyone, 

Today we’re going to make Chevreul's salt, a divalent copper complex. Divalent means it contains two different oxidation levels, in this case Cu +1, and Cu +2. Chevreul’s salt is also a deep, brick red color, and is insoluble in water. Apart from typical safety equiptment, you are going to need a good ventilation system. For our experiment you will need Copper Sulfate, and Sodium Metabisulfide. The two solutions were mixed, heated, and gravity filtered. The salt wasn't weighed, but it was enough to place in a dram vial.  I hope you all enjoyed the video, if you did comment down below!

title: Turning Lead into Gold: Golden Rain Experiment (Lead Iodide Synthesis)

title: Ampouling Liquid Chlorine
description:
DO NOT ATTEMPT
Chlorine is dangerous and high-pressure ampoules are generally unsafe.

Didn’t explain it much in the video but the chlorine remains a liquid as the ampoule returns to room temperature because the pressure inside the ampoule approaches 7 atmospheres. That’s why each ampoule is heat tested, to makes sure that the pressures necessary to keep chlorine liquid also don’t mean the vial is an explosive hazard.

title: How to Make Powerful High Voltage Capacitors
description:
How to make hand-rolled High Voltage capacitors for voltage multipliers, Marx generators, (small) tesla coils, and other HV projects.

A few things I didn't cover in the video:

While Mylar is a FANTASTIC High Voltage DC dielectric, it rapidly degrades when used in radio frequency applications (i.e. Tesla Coil primary capacitor banks). While these capacitors can be used in smaller designs with lesser energies, anyone planning on using hand rolled capacitors in a medium to large sized Tesla coil should replace Mylar with Polypropylene or Biaxially oriented polypropylene sheeting. These materials have excellent performance at radio frequencies and are what the best commercially made MMC banks are composed of.

Discord: https://discord.gg/JKHdNatRHa

Thank you Patreon Supporters!

title: Flaming Chemical Volcano : Ammonium Dichromate
description: In this video I give the classic demonstration of the ammonium dichromate volcano and discuss some of the chemistry behind it.
transcript: In this video, I'll be demonstrating the classic ammonium dichromate volcano reaction. This is ammonium dichromate right here. You can see there's a lot of nitrogen, hydrogen, and oxygen surrounding this chromium. Leaves the chromium in a very high oxidation state. If we rearrange this molecule, we can make nitrogen, water, and chrome three oxide. And this will be accompanied by the release of heat because of this because of the formation of this very stable nitrogen nitrogen triple bond, water, etc. In fact, it's so much heat that it's enough to overcome the activation energy of more ammonium dichromate, which means that this can basically exothermically decompose in a runaway type reaction without the need of any outside oxygen or anything else. You simply just get some of this hot enough, it forms these products. As long as enough of that heat is transferred back to this, you get more and more happening until the whole reaction's complete. Now, this reaction is kind of unique in that this solid that's produced comes out rather fluffy and it's fluffed up by the the production of these gases as well. So, it makes a sort of volcano-ish looking thing. So, uh I guess a picture's worth a thousand words, so let's go try it. So, I have here a small pile of ammonium dichromate, perhaps 15 to 20 g or so. And you can see it's a bright orange powder. It's hexavalent chromium, so obviously you don't want to touch it or get solutions of it on you or anything. Uh but we're going to render it fairly safe by this reaction. So, all we need to do is supply a little activation energy. dichromate volcano reaction because now we've got what looks sort of like a volcano of this chromium trioxide here. If you compare it to the milled stuff, you can see it's a slightly darker shade of green, but if I were to grind this up in a mortar and pestle, it would be the same shade of green. transcript: In this video, I'll be showing you how I get ethanol for my laboratory. Now, ethanol is a very useful and versatile solvent uh in the lab and it's it's used very widely in pretty much any laboratory. You'll find it everywhere. Now, it's it's come it comes in two major forms. The first form, of course, is just pure ethanol, which most accredited laboratories can buy. Unfortunately, I do not have access to that sort of thing. So, uh, I have to actually get mine from consumer products. And I have two choices. I can either buy it from buy it as denatured alcohol at the hardware store, which is mixed with things or denatured, which means you can't drink it. It's been, uh, its drinkable nature has been removed. Um, and this is done by typically adding a bunch of methanol, which is extremely poisonous. Um, I guess by my lab standards, not extremely poisonous, but it's it's quite poisonous. You certainly wouldn't want to drink it. Um, or I could buy drinking alcohol and try and isolate the alcohol, the ethanol that is from that. Um, the drinking alcohol route is kind of a bad route. I've tried this before and there's just so much water in it, you need a huge distillation apparatus in order to get any decent yield out of it. Uh, for a one liter flask, you're looking at probably 300 milliliters of of uh 90 orotropic ethanol, which is like 94ish% um, out of that, which is a pretty crappy yield. Now, this stuff that I found, this Clean Strip Green brand denatured alcohol is actually a very good substitute for that because this is almost completely pure ethanol. Now this is 80 to 90% ethanol. So says the MSDS with less than 5% methanol and 1.5% ethylacetate. Now that's interesting because ethylacetate as we know is an esther and esters are subject to hydraysis. So what we can do is mix this stuff with sodium hydroxide which will convert the ethylacetate into sodium acetate and more ethanol. And then uh remove the water with say potassium carbonate or something that'll suck it up. Molecular sibs, whatever you want. And then we'll be left with substantially pure ethanol with uh still less than 5% methanol. It'll end up if it was at 5%, it'll end up at like uh three and change percent if uh the MSDS is correct. And we've removed the water and the ethylacetate, which is very very good as far as denatured alcohol goes. That means that uh if you were to say make an ethylester um less than 1 in 25 molecules that you'd make of the ethylester would end up being the methylaster assuming they were equally selective uh in the reaction if you use this rather than say straight from drinking alcohol. And that frankly for my lab it's good enough for me. So anyway, I'm going to uh go ahead and do that with this container of denatured alcohol and show you where I get my ethanol from. title: The Purification of Ethanol from Drugstore Ethyl Rubbing Alcohol
description: In this video, I will purify ethanol from drugstore ethyl rubbing alcohol through distillation.  190 mL of 70% ethanol will be distilled to remove most of the water and many of the impurities.  Then, the ethanol will be treated with anhydrous copper sulfate to thoroughly dry it.  The product is about 125 mL of reasonably pure and dry ethanol.  It is not drinkable because there are still small amounts of denaturing agents in the ethanol.  Thanks for watching, and see you next time on Paradox Home Chemistry.

title: Chlorobenzene from Aniline via the Sandmeyer Reaction
description:
Chlorobenzene is a useful chemical in organic syntheses. It dissolves a wide range of organic compounds, making it an effective high-boiling solvent. It also has numerous applications as a reagent. For example, it is a direct precursor to phenylmagnesium chloride, a valuable Grignard reagent that can be used to produce a variety of compounds (e.g. benzene, triphenylmethanol, etc.). 

The most common laboratory preparation of chlorobenzene involves the direct chlorination of benzene using a Lewis acid catalyst. This preparation is considerably hazardous, as it involves the use of very toxic and carcinogenic chemicals. For home chemists, a safer method of preparing chlorobenzene is the use of the Sandmeyer reaction. This reaction entails the diazotization of aniline to a phenyldiazonium salt

Radioactive material should be stored in something like glass or lead to prevent/weaken the rays.

title: Lighting Ice on Fire
description:
In this video I add Calcium Carbide to an excess of Ice, the ice melts and produces acetylene gas which is then ignited to create a pretty cool illusion. This illusion makes the ice appear as if it is on fire! 

When the reaction is completed sodium hydroxide is a byproduct left in the container, and the acetylene has been combusted to produce water and carbon dioxide.

title: Making Thermite Out Of Batteries (But This Time It Actually Works)
description:
In this video I revisit my viral video known as "How To Make Thermite Out Of Batteries". The reaction I produced in that video is probably around 1 million times weaker than the reactions in this video. I added an extra ingredient to the formula I used in the first video and it worked flawlessly. I use the manganese dioxide that was recovered from the batteries in the original video and then some purchased barium nitrate as well as aluminum powder.

title: A VERY Reactive Chemical...
description:
I mix sulfuric acid (H2SO4) with potassium permanganate (KMnO4) to make manganese heptoxide (Mn2O7)! I play around with this extremely reactive, yet quite intriguing chemical. I make it decompose with lasers, convert to a beautiful purple permanganic acid, and of course, pour highly flammable liquids onto it. 
Manganese heptoxide is a chemical that just doesn't want to be around. It looks for any chance it can to decompose. When it comes into contact with nearly any organic substance it catches on fire.

title: Making Different Colors of Plasma
description:
In this video I use a microwave to make and capture plasma. I did not just want the standard yellow plasma though, I wanted multiple different colors of it. To make the different colors I forced certain ions to the plasma state. I got salts of Barium, Lithium and Potassium and made solutions out of them and sprayed the inside of my beaker in the microwave. 

Please do not try this at home, it can harm you and the microwave. 
(Not to mention my 3 broken beakers and watch glasses lol)

title: How To Make Thermite Out Of Pennies
description:
I don't know why you'd want to make thermite out of pennies but if you're ever bored or feeling adventurous... now you can! Pennies made before 1981 are made out of copper and when copper is heated up, it oxidizes. Once you have a metal oxide, you can usually make thermite with it. In this particular video I mixed the copper oxide from the pipes and pennies with aluminum powder and boom, thermite! 

I hope you enjoyed the video!

TWITTER: https://www.twitter.com/techience

title: Extracting the Active Ingredient from Aspirin
description:
In this video I extract the active ingredient (acetylsalicylic acid C9H8O4) from Aspirin! I use acetone as a solvent but you can use ethanol too. The reason I want to do this is because its fun, and acetylsalicylic acid is a useful chemical that I may need for future projects. I had a 68% yield which isn't bad considering I spilled some.

title: Synthesis of Fluorescein and demonstration of total internal reflection
description:
In this video, we explore the fascinating world of fluorescence and total internal reflection by synthesizing fluorescein and demonstrating its unique properties. Fluorescein is a fluorescent compound that emits bright green light when exposed to UV light. We take you through the synthesis process step-by-step and show you how to purify and characterize the compound.

Once we have our purified fluorescein, we use it to demonstrate total internal reflection, a phenomenon that occurs when light is reflected back into a medium rather than passing through it. By setting up a simple optical setup with a prism and a laser, we show how fluorescein can be used to visualize the critical angle of total internal reflection and create stunning optical effects.

title: How to make Alcohol(Ethanol) from sugar and yeast
description:
In this video, you will learn how to make alcohol using sugar and yeast. Sugar is an essential ingredient in the fermentation process as it provides the necessary energy for the yeast to convert sugar into alcohol. This video will guide you through the process of making alcohol step-by-step, from preparing the ingredients to the fermentation and bottling. You will also learn tips and tricks to ensure the best possible outcome for your homemade alcohol.

title: Laboratory preparation of hydrogen gas
description:
In the video, the laboratory preparation of hydrogen gas is demonstrated using zinc granules and dilute hydrochloric acid. This method is commonly known as the zinc and hydrochloric acid reaction.

The experiment begins with the setup of a round-bottom flask or a gas collection tube, which is filled with dilute hydrochloric acid. The acid is typically prepared by adding a specific volume of concentrated hydrochloric acid to a larger volume of water. The concentration of the acid may vary depending on the specific requirements of the experiment.

Zinc granules are then added to the flask or tube containing the dilute hydrochloric acid. The zinc granules react with the hydrochloric acid to produce hydrogen gas according to the following chemical equation:

Zn(s) + 2HCl(aq) → ZnCl2(aq) + H2(g

title: How to make Nitrobenzene from Benzene

We also discuss the properties and applications of diethyl ether, including its use as a solvent, fuel, and starting material for the sy

title: How To Make a DIY Fractionating Column | Fractional Distillation
description:
In this video, I demonstrate how to build a fractionating column from scratch using simple materials that are easily available. A fractionating column is a type of distillation apparatus that separates a mixture of liquids into its individual components based on their boiling points. It is commonly used in chemistry labs and in the production of alcoholic beverages.

In this tutorial, I show step-by-step how to construct a fractionating column, including the materials needed, tools required, and safety precautions to take. I explain the science behind how the column works and walk through the assembly process, so you can easily follow along and build one for yourself.

By the end of this video, you'll have a functional DIY fractionating column that you can use for distilling your own liqui
transcript: used to separate a mixture of volatile liquids on basis of their boiling points for example we take a mixture of methanol in water of 10 is to 90 ratio and then we just simply distill it we will have a mixture of 50 methanol to obtain pure methanol we have to distill again and again until we get 100 methanol so to simplify this we can use a fractionating column instead of distilling again and again so I will just briefly explain how a fractionating column Works consider we have a mixture of 10 percent methanol in water when we boil it the composition of vapor will be 50 percent according to the diagram shown in the right then it travels through the column where it partially condenses and falls back thus a temperature gradient is established inside the column from bottom to top and the column allows for the vapor to interact with the refluxing liquid and thus an equilibrium is attained now as the vapor travels up its composition of methanol will increase and finally if there are sufficient theoretical plates we will achieve hundred percent methanol vapor at the top of the column there are various types of fractionating columns and efficient column is the one that can give better separation this can be done by increasing the number of theoretical plates this can be done either by increasing the length of the column or by increasing the surface area now these are three common types of fractionating columns the one on the left is the vigrex column which is least efficient among the three the middle one is the deftone column it is designed in a way that the vapor has to travel through the spiral to reach the top thus increasing the surface area of contact of vapor with reflexing liquid it has very good efficiency now the third type is the packed columns which consist of a hollow glass tube which can be packed either with steel wool or glass beads these columns have maximum efficiency and give best separation so if one doesn't have fractionating column one can pack his libix condenser with steel wool like I did in my alcohol making video and from the 500 mL 12 percent alcohol solution we got only 70 mL of 61 percent ethanol then I purchased a vigrex column and again made ethanol the same way but without yeast so this time on distilling I got 72 mL of 86 percent ethanol solution so I studied about fractionating columns and decided to make my own packed column for that I took a steel tube 1.8 centimeter in diameter Teflon tape a B24 joint of a broken RB flask and stainless steel scrubbers so first with the help of a hacksaw I cut 45 centimeter of the tube to pack the steel wool first I stretch the scrubber like this and then cut it to open it now it can be fitted inside the tube inserted the steel wool inside the pipe then with the long iron Rod I packed the steel wool in the column I bought four scrubbers but my column could accumulate only three of them once the column was packed from top to bottom I wrapped Teflon tape at the bottom leaving some space for the drip tip I read the tape until it didn't become thick enough to form a Tight Seal with the B24 socket having done that I wrapped some Teflon tape at the top of the column so that I can fix my B24 socket for placing my steelhead in it as you can see here how I have fixed it but when I was adjusting it it broke as it was cracked so I purchased an enhancing adapter of cone size b19 and socket size B24 and as you can see I have wrapped the column with cotton and used thread to keep the cotton in place I have done this because for column to reach its in this video we will be making thymolphthalein  .it is an acid base indicator which is blue in alkaline medium .

thymol and phthalic anhydride are heated in a boiling water bath in presence of concentrated sulphuric acid.

title: Synthesis of Congo Red ( an old acid base indicator)
description:
Congo red is a synthetic diazo dye that is commonly used in histology to stain amyloid deposits in tissues. Its vibrant red color and ability to selectively bind to amyloid fibrils make it a valuable tool in medical research for identifying and studying various amyloid-related diseases, such as Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Congo red has applications in the textile industry as a dye for cotton, wool, and silk, due to its intense red hue and resistance to fading. With its versatility and importance in both scientific and industrial fields, Congo red continues to play a significant role in various areas of study and application.

Step into the world of organic chemistry with this detailed video showcasing the synthesis of Congo red. Follow along as skilled chemists combine precise reage

title: How to make 99% Fuming Nitric Acid from drain cleaner
description:
The laboratory preparation of nitric acid typically involves the reaction of a nitrate salt with concentrated sulfuric acid, followed by distillation to obtain the nitric acid. The process can be summarized in the following steps:

A nitrate salt, such as sodium nitrate (NaNO3) or potassium nitrate (KNO3), is mixed with concentrated sulfuric acid (H2SO4) in a reaction vessel. The mixture is typically cooled during the addition of the nitrate salt to control the exothermic reaction.

The reaction between the nitrate salt and sulfuric acid produces nitric acid (HNO3), nitrogen dioxide gas (NO2), and water vapor (H2O): NaNO3 + H2SO4 → HNO3 + NaHSO4 2HNO3 → 2NO2 + H2O + O2

The nitrogen dioxide gas and water vapor are removed by passing the gas through a condenser or scrubber, which cools the

In the video, viewers learn how to mix luminol with hydrogen peroxide and a catalyst (such as potassium hydroxide) in the presence of oxygen. This combination triggers an oxidation reaction that results in the emission of light, demonstrating the principle of chemiluminescence. The video likely explains the science behind the reaction, including the role of the catalyst in facilitating the reaction and the energy released during the process. title: Determination of total hardness in water
description:
Determination of total hardness in water involves measuring the concentration of dissolved minerals, primarily calcium (Ca²⁺) and magnesium (Mg²⁺) ions, which contribute to the hardness. The total hardness is typically expressed in terms of calcium carbonate (CaCO₃) equivalents.

The most common method to determine total hardness is the EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid) titration method. Here's how it generally works:

Sample Preparation: A water sample is collected for analysis.

pH Adjustment: The sample’s pH is adjusted to around 10 using a buffer solution to ensure that the metal ions (Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺) are in a state that will react with the titrant.

Indicator Addition: A chemical indicator, such as Eriochrome Black T, is added. This indicator forms a color complex with calcium and

title: Determination of Chloride ions in Tap Water
description:
The determination of chloride ions in tap water is typically carried out using a method called titration, where a solution of known concentration (called the titrant) is added to the water sample until a reaction is complete. One of the most common methods for this is Mohr's method, which uses silver nitrate (AgNO₃) as the titrant and potassium chromate (K₂CrO₄) as the indicator. Here’s a basic overview of the process:

Sample Preparation: A known volume of the tap water sample is taken.

Addition of Potassium Chromate: A few drops of potassium chromate (which acts as an indicator) are added to the sample. The chromate ion (CrO₄²⁻) is yellow in color and will form a reddish-brown precipitate in the presence of silver ions (Ag⁺).

Titration with Silver Nitrate: The water sample is titrated

title: Gravimetric Analysis of Phosphate in Coca-Cola
description:
Gravimetric analysis is a quantitative method used to determine the amount of a substance by measuring the mass of its stable, insoluble precipitate. In the context of determining phosphate content in Coca-Cola, this method involves precipitating phosphate ions as a solid compound, isolating it, and measuring its mass to calculate the phosphate concentration.​

Real-life examples and applications

title: Making Phenyl Propiolic acid

This video takes you on a fascinating journey into the world of organic chemistry as we synthesize phenyl propiolic acid, a versatile building block with applications in pharmaceuticals, dyes, and materials science. We'll start with cinnamic acid, a naturally occurring compound found in cinnamon, and guide you through the multi-step process of converting it into this valuable alkyne.
This two-step process, starting with bromination and followed by a double elimination, is a well-established route in organic chemistry.
​Step 1: Bromination of Cinnamic Acid

title: Making Bromine
description:
In this video, we're diving into the complex chemistry behind creating one of the world's most unique elements: liquid bromine. We'll use a powerful combination of manganese dioxide, potassium bromide, and sulfuric acid to perform this synthesis. The video will walk you through two distinct approaches: the traditional dry method and the more modern wet method, highlighting the key differences and challenges of each. Finally, we'll demonstrate how to properly and safely collect and seal our newly synthesized bromine into a glass ampule for long-term storage.

title: Performing the Ugi Reaction
description:
The Ugi reaction is a powerful, one-pot, four-component condensation (U-4CC) reaction in organic chemistry.
​Key Features of the Ugi Reaction
​Components: It combines four different reactants in a single step:
​An aldehyde or ketone (carbonyl compound)
​An amine
​A carboxylic acid
​An isocyanide
​Product: The reaction produces a single product, an \alpha-acylamino amide (also known as a bis-amide).
​Mechanism: The reaction proceeds through a cascade of steps, including the initial formation of an iminium ion, followed by a nucleophilic addition of the isocyanide, and a final, irreversible rearrangement called a Mumm rearrangement, which drives the reaction to completion.
​Significance: It is highly valued in drug discovery and combinatorial chemistry because its multi-component nature allo

title: Performing the Reimer Tiemann Reaction
description:
Reimer–Tiemann reaction, a classic organic chemistry reaction used to convert phenol into salicylaldehyde using chloroform (CHCl₃) and aqueous sodium hydroxide (NaOH). This experiment demonstrates the formation of a formyl group (–CHO) on the aromatic ring through the generation of the highly reactive dichlorocarbene intermediate.
The video shows the complete experimental procedure step-by-step, including preparation of the reaction mixture, controlled heating, acidification, and isolation of the final product. You will also learn about the reaction mechanism, why the aldehyde group forms mainly at the ortho position, and how this reaction is important in aromatic chemistry.
This practical experiment is very useful for B.Sc., M.Sc., and competitive exam students (CSIR-NET, GATE, etc.) who

I could have used sodium hydroxide instead of potassium hydroxide but after reading a research paper I found that potassium hydroxide gives a better yield than sodium hydroxide. title: Dissolving an aluminum can in sodium hydroxide
description:
I've had several failures recently, so for lack of a better video, I threw this together today. About 25g of sodium hydroxide was dissolved in ~100ml of water. An aluminum can was dropped in and allowed to react, producing sodium aluminate and hydrogen gas. The hydrogen kept building up under the can and lifting it out, I should have poured the NaOH solution inside but oh well. 

I'm working on the carbothermal reduction of sodium sulfate to Na2S right now, my best yield is around 40%. I think I need more heat! The next video might be on making phenol, I don't know I'm lazy we'll see what comes next.

title: Reaction of sodium persulfate with halide salts
description:
In this video I react sodium persulfate with NaCl, NaBr, and KI. As the size of the atom increases so does the ease of removing electrons because the valence electrons are further away from the nucleus. An electron has to be removed from the halide to give the free halogen, so iodide is the easiest to oxidize and fluoride is the hardest.

The bromide and iodide were oxidized to Br2 and I2, respectively. With NaCl the was no apparent reaction. I couldn't smell and chlorine and there was no green color. There was a bit of bubbling but I think that was just decomposition of the Na2S2O8. Perhaps it needs to be acidified. 

Warning: Do not breathe in bromine or iodine vapors. Do not get bromine or iodine on your skin. Sodium bromide, potassium iodide and sodium persulfate are harmful when inges

title: Burning trimethyl borate: green flames!
description:
I made some triemthyl borate today and I thought I'd show it burning. It's interesting because boron gives flames a green color, and B(OCH3)3 is flammable. 

Word of warning to anyone who makes it, B(OCH3)3 hydrolyzes very easily in air. If the ground glass joints are not greased then it will leak out in small amounts and a white film will begin to cover the outside of your glassware and the area around the distillation apparatus. Protect the final product from moisture.

Also I know I spelled hydrolyze wrong in the video but in my defense I am a horrible speller. I'm also too lazy to fix it.

title: Chemical Rainbow
description: A few of the colors of the rainbow using some chemicals I had. It's pretty bad, I challenge someone else (I wonder who actually watches these videos) to make a better one using as many chemicals and colors as possible. I'll work on version 2.0 for the future.

title: Homemade fume hood
description: This is a short video of my sweet new fume hood. I built it with my dad over the weekend for a total cost of ~$210. The sash is made of Plexiglass and the front swings up on hinges for easy setup. It has power and lights, and eventually will have ports in the side for water + vacuum.

title: New Chemglass Vacuum Pump!
description:
Sorry that it's not a lab video, I'm really busy and I think I'll get around to posting one next week. This is my new Chemglass vacuum pump! All of the seals and internals are fluoropolymer, so you don't have to worry about corrosion. There's no oil so pumping water or solvent vapor is no problem! If you are looking to get a pump for filtration or vacuum distillation of medium boiling compounds (under 250C) then this is the pump for you! It's rated to go down to 50 torr although it can get a bit lower.

This is the one: http://www.chemglass.com/product_view.asp?pnr=CG-4812-10

title: Reaction of Copper with Nitric Acid
description: An old but fun reaction. Nitric acid is a strong acid and a strong oxidizer. It oxidizes copper metal to copper 2+ and is itself reduced to nitrogen dioxide. The loud sound in the background is my fume hood.
safety: concentrated nitric acid is corrosive and oxidizing
