title: Preparation of Barium Nitrate
description:
Warning: barium salts are toxic and concentrated nitric acid is extremely corrosive. In this video I prepare barium nitrate by the reaction of barium hydroxide with nitric acid, a simple acid-base reaction. The yield is pretty good at 98% based on hydroxide.  

Organic reactions coming soon I promise.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing barium nitrate by the reaction of barium hydroxide octa hydrate with nitric acid to that of barium carbonate there's no bubbling and no mist of soluble barium salts in the air I weighed out 25 point to 4 grams of barium hydroxide octa hydrate into the speaker and I've measured out roughly 11 milliliters of concentrated nitric acid into this graduated cylinder the addition of some water made into a slurry and will help control the temperature when I add the nitric acid texture of the crystals in it have changed and the mixture is now acid with a pH of about 1/2 pH paper they're still solid because barium nitrate actually isn't super soluble of water I'll now proceed to turn the hot plate onto a medium setting and let it slowly evaporate after a couple of hours on the hot plate I'm left with a dry crystalline solid which is barium nitrate in the end I'm left with twenty point four eight grams of barium nitrate which corresponds to a ninety-eight percent yield

title: Benzopinacol Preparation: a Photochemical Reaction
description:
Benzophenone dissolved in 2-propanol undergoes a reductive coupling reaction (pinacol coupling actually) in the presence of sunlight. The melting point of the product is variable, with literature values ranging from 170 to 195 C. Considering the chemicals involved and the conditions, I think a MP of 173-177 C is enough for identification. If I wasn't heating so fast I think the range would be sharper.

Reference: Organic Syntheses, Coll. Vol. 2, p.71 (1943);
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing benzo pinnacle by the coupling of two molecules of benzophenone this is a photochemical reaction and will take place in the presence of sunlight I weighed out ten point nine grams of benzophenone into this flask and now I will add 70 milliliters of dry isopropanol which to which has been added one drop of dry acetic acid after a bit of stirring and a little bit more isopropanol because I was impatient I've dissolved all of the benzophenone and I will now place this Stoppard flask in the sunny spot I can find and we just spent five days since that last video in that time I've tried to have this flask in sunlight for as long as possible during the day at best it's had a few hours of sunlight direct sunlight every day and it should go longer but I'm impatient so I'll do the workup now

title: Choline Chloride from Choline Bitartrate (New)
description: Edit of the original with added narration. In this video I prepare choline chloride from a dietary supplement, choline bitartrate. Thanks to aga for getting me to add speaking. The first was was a little bland.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing choline chloride by the reaction of choline bitartrate and potassium chloride the two reactants were weighed out in equal molar amounts next they were both dissolved in water the choline solution was cloudy so it was filtered the two solutions were combined immediately precipitating a white solid the filtrate boiled down at negative 10 Celsius more solid was formed more boiling down followed before the solution was placed in an oven choline chloride started to decompose transferred to a bottle her storage and leader-goose [Music]

title: Benzaldehyde Preparation Using Nitric Acid
description:
In this video I prepare benzaldehyde with a yield of 72% by the oxidation of benzyl alcohol with nitric acid. The product can be vacuum distilled to remove the red color and purify further, I didn't do this because the impurity won't interfere with my next reaction and that product will be distilled.

Where I found out about this method: http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=72076
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing benzaldehyde by the oxidation of benzyl alcohol using nitric acid and sodium nitrite as a catalyst in this video I will use 31.1 milliliters of benzyl alcohol 13.1 milliliters of concentrated nitric acid one gram of sodium nitrite and 100 milliliters of water it would also be useful to have a large amount of sodium hydroxide on hand I have measured out our three reactants here is the concentrated nitric acid here's the benzyl alcohol and here's the water first I'll add the water this is 98 milliliters to the flask the concentrated nitric acid you dissolved in approximately two milliliters of water and I will now add this with rapid stirring slowly to the clasp in the mixture as the nitrous acid that's produced decomposes a little bit it won't happen too much but it is important to have ventilation now I'll add the benzyl alcohol water so it makes very small droplets and this is what makes the liquid look cloudy I will now add a condenser and hook that up large amount of sodium hydroxide in water and I set up a tube leading from the top of the condenser into this inverted funnel during the reaction a lot of nitrogen dioxide and nitrogen monoxide will be produced so it's important to have something to neutralize the gases that come out now that I have my trap set up I will start strong stirring now currently at around 85 Celsius and you can see the mixture is darkened and around 70 Celsius it started to bubble and I'm gonna try and keep this flask between 90 degrees Celsius and a low boil the paper suggests that I should keep it at 90 Celsius but I can't really achieve that good temperature control with the heating mantle like this so I'll just try to keep it as close to 90 as possible and if not that then over a little bit as you can see the mixture is dark into an orange and the rate of bubbles coming out has slowed down but it still happens been 3 hours and 40 minutes and the gas production slowed down to almost nothing so I decided it was time to stop this is the bottom layer it has been transferred to a separatory funnel and washed with a bit of distilled water I will now wash it with a sodium bicarbonate solution until it stopped bubbling and then I will wash it with a sodium chloride solution to dry red orange liquid which if you if you want carefully towards yourself as delicious aroma of almonds or maraschino cherries and Avis benz outcome subtracting a little bit for water impurities because it's not totally dry I would say you can get 23 grams of its aldehyde from this that is a 72 point two percent yield it's not as good as the paper that I was reading from but suggest but then again I didn't control the temperature very well and I didn't follow the instructions exactly it make it easier for myself so that's not bad I'm not drawing it completely because I will combine it with some other Wenzel on how to have to do a final purification before I use it for another reaction that will come up in another video soon

title: Abderhalden's Drying Pistol
description: I demonstrate the use of a drying pistol.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be showing the use of abdur Aldens drying pistol a piece of glassware that is similar to but has many distinct differences from a vacuum desiccator it is made up of two parts the main body is shown here notice the inner and outer glass tube on the right the inner tube is where the sample to be dried is placed the outer tube is connected to the bottom and top joint a flask is placed on the bottom joint and a condenser is placed on the top here is the drying chamber it fits on the end of the other piece of glassware notice the ridge this is to keep the drying agent in the chamber it also has a stopcock to control the flow of gas out of the apparatus the joint is rather large in order to fit a large sample into the drying chamber I will now set up to dry something I've placed water in the bottom flask to dry the sample at 100 Celsius I have placed concentrated sulfuric acid in the drying chamber to act as the drying agent and I have placed a Liebig condenser on top because water is not that volatile you can put any liquid you like in the flask below to determine the temperature at which your sample will dry here I have run a line going to my vacuum pump to ensure that most of the air is removed the stopcock was then turned to contain the vacuum and the sample is left to dry under heating for as long as you deem necessary alternative to vacuum drying ovens because the sample is heated it can be dried much faster than in a vacuum desiccator at room temperature its drawback is that known as large samples can be placed into the drying chamber

title: A Bromine Halloween
description: Happy Halloween everyone!
safety: bromine is highly toxic and corrosive

title: Making Phthalic Anhydride from Phthalic Acid
description: I make phthalic anhydride from phthalic acid by heating.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing phthalic anhydride from phthalic acid the beaker seen here contains approximately 60 grams of phthalic acid it's placed on a hot plate and the hot plate is turned out to medium-high heat as it began to heat up I placed a piece of aluminum foil in a metal pan and set it aside for later when I want to collect my product while that's going on I'll talk a little bit about the reaction for most carboxylic acids it is impossible to form the acid anhydride just by heating but phthalic acid is special because it contains two acid groups which are adjacent to each other the formation of phthalic anhydride actually forms a ring the ring is quite stable and this makes the formation of phthalic anhydride much more favorable compared to other acid anhydride because of this when you heat a phthalic acid to around 180 Celsius it begins to dehydrate giving off water and becoming phthalic anhydride eventually it will stop giving off water and completely melt signaling the end of reaction you can see water condensing on the walls of the beaker as this reaction takes place state water was no longer being emitted an evaporating dish was placed on top to contain the subliming phthalic anhydride the solid then melted and we poured it out onto the piece of aluminum foil to collect the product solidified and was broken up and placed into a bottle for storage to phthalic anhydride

title: Preparation of 1-bromobutane
description:
I prepare 1-bromobutane from n-butanol and hydrobromic acid.


Note:  I used 17.5 mL HBr, not 18.5. For some reason I typed the wrong number.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing one bromo butane I will use eleven milliliters of an butanol 7.4 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid and two 17.5 milliliters of concentrated hydrobromic acid 50 milliliter round bottom flask adding 4.1 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid flask in an ice bath doesn't need to be kept cold but I just left it in the ice bath while I did this concentrated sulfuric acid the flask was lowered into my oversized heating mantle and a condenser was placed on top this is preparing for the reflux that will take place next and the heating mantle was turned on once the contents of the flask started to boil I started the clock and reflux the mixture for three hours heating and let the flask cool to room temperature you could see that two layers formed to the upper layer being our product I set the flask up for symbol distillation to recover the bromo butane the mixture was distilled until no more oily and cloudy drops came over after this the distillate separated into two layers and there was only one layer present in the boiling flask butane it was then washed with water hydrochloric acid water sodium bicarbonate solution and lastly water these steps aren't included because I have a feeling that all of my viewers know how to use a separatory funnel bottom layer was transferred to a vial containing some calcium chloride to dry it as you can see after a few hours it had cleared up completely milliliter round bottom flask and a stir bar was added unfortunately I spilled a bunch and got some calcium chloride into the flask product came over between 95 and 98 Celsius this boiling point is almost 10 degrees too low and I attribute this to my crappy thermometer given the properties of the liquid and the chemicals we put into the reaction there's really nothing this could be other than our desired product the final yield was seven point nine six grams or 48.3% the disappointing yield is a combination of small scale and me spilling a bunch into my heating mantle during a transfer step this has been the preparation of 1-bromo butane thanks for watching and don't forget to subscribe

title: Making a Choline Chloride/Urea Deep Eutectic Solvent
description: I make a liquid by mixing two solids, choline chloride and urea. This mixture is known as a eutectic, and in this case the eutectic temperature is 12 C, making it liquid.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing a deep eutectic solvent first and hydrous choline chloride and urea were mixed together in a 1 to 2 molar ratio inside a glass vial the vial was placed on a hot plate turned to low heat the mixture slowly began to melt and once it was liquid enough I added a stir bar to help it mix together over the course of an hour nearly all of the solid melted and we were left with a completely liquid mixture temperature the contents remained liquid this is interesting because the melting point of urea is about 130 Celsius and the melting point of choline chloride is over 300

title: Making Diethyl Phthalate
description: I prepare diethyl phthalate from phthalic anhydride, ethanol, and sulfuric acid. Video clip of a chemiluminescent reaction is from a NurdRage video, “Make Glow Sticks - The Science.”
transcript: lab in this video i'll be making diethyl phthalate this chemical is used as a solvent in glowsticks first I measured out sixteen point two five grams of phthalic anhydride and transferred it to a 250 milliliter flask this was followed by a hundred and fifty milliliters of ethanol which was used to wash the funnel out a boiling stone was added a stir bar would also have been fine finally I added six milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid over the flask I set up a Sox lit extractor which was packed with 15 grams of three a molecular sieves water was started to the condenser and I attached a drying tube to the top of it for two hours and distilled off the excess ethanol I recovered about a hundred and ten milliliters of ethanol before calling it good and stopping the distillation water sodium bicarbonate solution until it stopped bubbling and then water again the cloudy bottom layer was dried within hydrous magnesium sulfate finally I filtered off the drying agent the yield was fifteen point seven seven grams a 65% yield based off of phthalic anhydride because of its high boiling point I will distill it under vacuum in a future video I used too much drying agent - there were many transfer losses as you can see the droplets here in my waste and an incident with a separatory funnel that involved my product spraying onto the bench

title: Diacetone alcohol Synthesis
description:
I prepare diacetone alcohol from acetone using barium hydroxide as a catalyst. The product is interesting because it is both a ketone and an alcohol. I will use this in a future video to make mesityl oxide.

Procedure from Vogel's Textbook of Practical Organic Chemistry, Fifth Edition, page 802.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing for methyl for hydroxy Penton to own better known as dye acetone alcohol I will make it by the base catalyzed condensation of two acetone molecules a thimble packed with barium hydroxide octa hydrate was placed in a soxhlet extractor through which was run 130 milliliters of acetone this should be left to run until the contents of the flask boil at greater than 100 Celsius 80 to 100 hours is normal time for this excess acetone was then distilled off vapor temperature went above 70 Celsius to 40 milliliters left in the Sox lid heating to 60 Celsius under vacuum removed most of the remaining acetone flask and vacuum distilled the product was collected between 85 and 95 Celsius at around 30 Torr a small amount of acetone water for run came over it's worth being noted that not all of the product could be transferred to the flask alcohol the low yield is because my sock slit was only run for 35 hours however all of the unchanged acetone can be reused

title: Reduction of Copper Oxide with Hydrogen
description: I made copper metal from copper oxide by reducing the latter with hydrogen. Oxides of less reactive metals can all be reduced using hydrogen and I will show this process with a more interesting metal oxide in future.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be reducing copper oxide to copper metal using hydrogen gas this method works on a variety of metal oxides and I will show a more interesting example in a future video first I'll show you my apparatus it starts with a 250 milliliter flask with lumen amande water above it is an addition funnel with sodium hydroxide solution this leads to a calcium chloride drying tube after that tubing leads to a fused quartz tube with it has some copper oxide powder in the middle the two vents to the atmosphere I also had a Bunsen burner below the tube to start the experiment I started adding sodium hydroxide solution to the flask it took a little messing around but eventually I got hydrogen to be produced a pretty constant rate to test that hydrogen was actually coming out I put a test tube over the output and lit it I through the apparatus before lighting it on the output I then lit my Bunsen burner and started heating the copper oxide with it the hydrogen reduces it to copper metal and is oxidized into water you can see the water collecting in the tube farther on I'm not sure exactly how long you need to heat it but I did this for about 2 minutes and that seemed ok I let the copper cool under a stream of hydrogen molar hydrochloric acid this dissolves any leftover copper oxide and leaves only copper metal

title: Purification of Toluene from the Hardware Store
description:
I purify some toluene, removing thiophenes and non-volatile impurities. Some molecular sieves can bring down water concentration to only a few ppm.

Distillation music from www.bensound.com
transcript: lab in this video I will be purifying hardware-store toluene most toluene contains some thiophenes which can't be removed by distillation I will use sulfuric acid to remove them I first measured out 250 milliliters of the toluene to this I added 25 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid the separatory funnel was then shaken well to wash the toluene this washing was continued with fresh acid until the acid didn't turn yellow after shaking this was another two times in my case distilled water drying over anhydrous potassium carbonate should clear up the toluene I got impatient and went straight to distillation the water can then be removed as a low boiling azeotrope leaving relatively dry toluene to be distilled over [Music] bottle of dry thiophene free toluene ready for lab use

title: Magnesium Nitride from Magnesium and Nitrogen
description:
I make magnesium nitride by heating magnesium in an atmosphere of nitrogen. 

The nitrogen generator is from Astral Chemistry, check out the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watchv=DlkpPYWhUI0&t=505s
transcript: video i'll be making magnesium nitride from magnesium metal and nitrogen gas ammonium chloride three point five six grams of sodium nitrite and about point five grams of magnesium i first added the ammonium chloride and sodium nitrite to a flask along with around hundred milliliters of water the mixture was stirred until everything dissolved chloride drawing tube which was connected to a fused quartz glass tube in the middle I placed magnesium chips although magnesium powder would be much better this led to a second drying tube and finally to a flask of water to monitor gas generation when I was ready I turned on heat to the flask nitrogen was slowly produced and eventually reached a good rate I let the apparatus purge for around 10 minutes to remove any oxygen after this I let my Bunsen burner and began to heat the magnesium it started to take on a black color a dark film then slowly spread over the glass magnesium very hot and let the tube cool under a flow of nitrogen some interesting patterns formed in the glass I was able to dislodge the chips with the a of a stirring rod the pieces are black and green but of white patches of magnesium oxide there also appears to be magnesium metal underneath magnesium powder would have worked much better I held a wet pH paper over the pieces as I added water magnesium nitride makes ammonia with water and the pH paper would detect any bubbling and a blue-green color indicated ammonia also noticed the milky color of the water from the magnesium hydroxide something interesting was observed when I tried to clean my tube with hydrochloric acid I didn't expect it listen to this magnesium silicide with the glass which reacted with acid to make silane my quartz tube was permanently damaged

title: Making 4-Hydroxybenzoic acid from PABA
description: I make 4-hydroxybenzoic acid, and isomer of salicylic acid, from 4-aminobenzoic acid, better known as PABA. I first convert it to the diazo salt using sodium nitrite and sulfuric acid, then I decompose that with heat and acid to replace the N2+ with OH.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing the molecule that you see here for hydroxybenzoic acid this is actually quite similar to something else you might have heard of to hydroxybenzoic acid also known as salicylic acid now personally I find the process that I use in this video to be much more interesting than the product and that process is diazotization that process is where a primary amine reacts with nitrous acid in an acidic solution to produce this diazonium salt you see here and now this salt has a huge number of uses and you can make many different things starting with the diazonium salt but me in this video I will just decompose it to produce this molecule you see here also a quick word of warning diazonium salts can be explosive and obviously that's not something you want to happen in the lab so some things that can increase the risk of explosion are too much nitrite and working in not dilute enough conditions first I measured out three point four five grams of for amino benzoic acid to this I added 7.5 milliliters of water and slowly dripped in 5.5 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid able to add all the sulfuric acid I got this suspension that you see here at this point I decided to reduce my scale to one third of the original idea I mix it up and measured out one third of the total volume which you can see here I flask and washed it down with some water bringing the total volume up to around 20 milliliters I then measured out 0.6 grams of sodium nitrite in the test tube here and I added about 2 milliliters of water to dissolve it while I left that dissolving I set up an ice bath around my flask this reaction needs to take place between 0 & 5 degrees Celsius so I let it cool down as the sodium nitrate dissolved once I reached the right temperature I added the solution of sodium nitrite to the addition funnel and began addition I had to play with the speed a lot turning on and off but eventually got a good rate and could leave it going prepared a 50 percent by volume sulfuric acid solution eventually I added all the sodium nitrite solution to the flask and everything dissolved I then let it stir like this for about 15 minutes after that time I turned off stirring and let the flask sit here for five minutes I next put the acid solution on a hot plate and put the reaction mixture in an addition funnel above the flask I started stirring and gentle heating and slowly started dripping in the reaction mixture I kept it around 60 or 70 Celsius and you can see slowly bubbles started to be produced these bubbles are of course nitrogen gas the whole addition took about 10 minutes and it turned yellow and then eventually went a more colorless flask off and let it cool to room temperature I was then pleased to see that a bunch of crystals had formed and I proceeded to filter them off with vacuum filtration here which I left overnight to dry and got this in the morning I packed a bit into a capillary tube and took the melting point of it to be about 205 which is a little bit low but it's close enough that I can reasonably say that this is for hydroxybenzoic acid
safety: diazonium salts can be explosive if dry

title: Synthesis of 3-Pentanone
description: I prepare 3-Pentanone by the ketonic decarboxylation of calcium propanoate. Low yielding, but it works.
transcript: decarboxylation this is a reaction where two equivalents of carboxylic acid reacted high temperatures over a catalyst to give a ketone along with carbon dioxide and water its name comes from the fact that it is a decarboxylation that produces a ketone it just so happens that this reaction will also take place with these salts of carboxylic acids I believe this happens with all salts but it works best with calcium and barium ones this reaction used to be used to produce acetone in a reaction that is demonstrated by Nile red today I will heat calcium propionate to high temperatures to give three pentanone as well as a little bit of calcium carbonate I bought my calcium propionate online as a food preservative it is used to preserve bread and we don't around 18 grams and proceeded to add this to a large 50 milliliter test tube I did this because I didn't want to damage any of my nice flasks next I wrapped a distillation adapter with a generous amount of PTFE tape in order to make a good seal I then fit this adapter into the test tube and attached the rest of my apparatus this consists of a grue column a vacuum tank off adapter and a receiving flask the very column isn't necessary I just used it as an air condenser a water cooled condenser would work even better when I was ready I let my Bunsen burner and began to gently heat the test tube after a minute or so I began to see condensation on the glass with further heating vapours managed to reach the condenser receiving flask heating was continued until nothing more came over which took about 20 minutes let everything cool down to room temperature separated into two layers I removed the bottom layer with pipette and dried the remaining liquid using this setup I found the boiling point to be 101 degrees Celsius which is only one degree below the literature boiling point dividing the samples mass by its volume I found the density to be about point seven eight grams per milliliter given these various properties the product here is mostly three pentanone the yield was quite poor at only 13 percent while the yield can be improved somewhat it will always be on the low side for this type of reaction

title: Make Cyclopentanone from Adipic Acid
description: I make cyclopentanone, a cyclic ketone, from adipic acid. This is an example of ketonic decarboxylation. It differs from my previous video because it uses the acid and a small amount of base to catalyze the reaction.
transcript: lab in this video i'll be making cyclopentanone from adipic acid this is actually another example of a Katona decarboxylation but in this case the two acid groups are on the same molecule producing a cyclic compound the reaction uses a small amount of barium hydroxide as a catalyst my starting materials are 100 grams of a dybbuk acid and 5 grams of barium hydroxide octa hydrate the two powders were mixed together well and added to a 500 milliliter flask which had a thermometer well and a distillation adapter in it the adapter led to a condenser which worked when I hooked up my water lines properly my 1 liter heating mantle was turned on and the temperature of the flask ramped up slowly at around 150 degrees the acid started to melt and crystals of adipic acid is formed higher up on the flask some water distilled over over at around 260 degrees a small layer can be seen on top of the water with some difficulty between 290 and 295 degrees for the remainder of the reaction this took a few hours at this temperature there is a good rate of reaction and any higher will make a dip ik acid come over darken to a black color when I thought most of the acid was gone I turned off heating left than I thought and this will lead to lower yields I pipette it off a small bottom layer of water and added a few scoops of anhydrous potassium carbonate to the distillate this is a good drying agent because it not only absorbs water but it also reacts with any adipic acid present and pipetted off a bottom layer of water which had formed mantle with this fancy expensive cord the crude cyclopentanone was added to a 50 milliliter flask on top of this was attached a with a crew column which went to a distillation adapter a Liebig condenser a vacuum take-off adapter and finally a receiving flask first the come off was at ninety three point five degrees and this is actually an azeotrope of cyclopentanone and water this the temperature rose to 128 degrees and I started collection this was continued until 130 degrees when the still had temperature started to drop cyclopentanone this represents a 60% yield not too bad considering how much adipic acid was left in the flask from stopping too early

title: Homemade 1100 C Tube Furnace
description:
I build a tube furnace capable of reaching 1100 C (2000 F) for $30 using parts from the internet and the hardware store. My design was heavily inspired by garage chemist and the following thread on SM: 
http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=9705
transcript: building a tube furnace the heart of the furnace is this ceramic tube it's rated up to 1500 Celsius and measures 25 millimeters on the outside 20 millimeters on the inside and 200 millimeters long I also got some 22 gauge nichrome wire around the tube I tried to keep the spacing as close together as possible but I was rather uneven and as you can see near the end the spacing was very bad this is okay though because I will fix it as I put on the mortar the mortar is this high heat mortar it cost about five dollars for a large tube and it's rated up to 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit or 1,100 Celsius I painted it on with a brush the first coat is a thin one to keep all the wire in place and after that I added a second thicker coat to completely cover everything here you can see it after everything is cured I also added on these two solid copper wires to service connections as the mortar was carrying I made the body of the furnace four inch metal duct was caught a little bit less than 200 millimeters long and I kind of aluminum flashing to make the ends like this one you see here edge so I could fold it over and then clamp it with a hose clamp for insulation I got this ceramic fiber or ceramic wall which is rated up to 1,300 Celsius after wrapping insulation around the tube I put everything inside of the housing and clamped on the other end it took about one square foot of half-inch ceramic fiber in order to insulate everything well through the end and I might fix this later but for now it works a thermocouple inserted to the center I started my testing for the initial run I kept the voltage low at around thirty volts this is so I don't stress anything and allow any water that's still in the mortar to be driven off starting in the inside from inside reached 1,100 Celsius

title: Benzamide Preparation from Benzoic Acid
description:
I prepare benzamide using benzoic acid and urea, using a catalyst of boric acid. 

Special thanks to the members who posted on this Sciencemadness thread: http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=4201&page=4
transcript: welcome back to the lab today I'll be preparing Benza mind in aromatic amide of benzoic acid and ammonia as well as the reaction of benzoyl chloride with ammonia for today I'll react benzoic acid and urea with a boric acid catalyst to make bends amide bends mine doesn't have a large number of uses but I intend to use it to make aniline through the Hofmann rearrangement this opens up a pathway to an alene without having to go through nitro benzene I started off with 110 grams of benzoic acid to this I added one hundred and sixty two point five grams of urea five grams of boric acid it was then shaken well to mix the reactants evenly the flask was placed in a salt bath on top of a hot plate I put a thermometer in the middle of the mixture and cover the top with tinfoil the thermometer allows me to take the temperature of the milk everything melted into a clear liquid around 130 degrees with evolution of gas eventually it reached about 180 degrees Celsius and I just did the hot plate to try and keep it at this temperature it should be kept around this temperature for about two-and-a-half hours notice the solids inside which will have to be knocked down periodically the reaction was rather uneventful but eventually it finished and I took it off and let it cool it gave the solid you see here to isolate the Ben's mind I added about 150 milliliters of 5% ammonia solution I then heated it with stirring on my hot plate the ammonia will react to make the benzoic acid and boric acid that may be present more soluble upon reaching 95 degrees almost everything had dissolved and I let it cool crystals precipitated as it cooled liquid was carefully poured off of these crystals 50 milliliters of a dilute ammonia solution products remained so I poured off the liquid into another beaker upon cooling to room temperature over an hour and a half or so you can see the very nice crystals which formed here the liquid was poured off and fresh distilled water was added the crystals were mixed well with the water to wash them everything was then vacuum filtered and washed with more water filter and left out to dry for a few hours eventually I actually melted them to drive off any remaining water my final product was seventy one point six one grams of some very nice Ben's mind the melting point of 128 degrees Celsius confirmed its identity the yield of 66% is somewhat low but it should be more than enough for the preparation of an amine

title: Synthesis and Chemiluminescence of Lophine
description:
Everyone has heard of luminol, but lophine is a much less common and much easier to synthesize chemical. Its luminescence is not as good, but still really really cool. 

https://www.patreon.com/Tomslab/

Aussie Chemist: 
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC1zhLq-8SrS2Au3IV71u7Mw
Piperliam: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCH8ZTfvZcsKCGzZB1xhjMgg

Lophine:
https://thosci.com/synthesis-of-lophine/
http://illumina-chemie.de/synthese-und-chemolumineszenz-von-lophin-t3657.html
transcript: probably first think of luminal but have you heard of Lowe Fein Lowe Fein was first synthesized in 1877 by a Polish chemist twenty-five years before luminol was first synthesized since then it seems to have been largely forgotten about now many people have heard of it now and in fact there's no Wikipedia page on it this makes sense because it's luminescence is much less spectacular than other common ones like luminol or TCP oh but it is much easier to synthesize this synthesis will be one step using benzyl benzoate ammonium acetate in a solvent of acetic acid I've shown in a previous video how to make benzaldehyde and the synthesis of low féin is possible using only benzaldehyde and ammonia to start I added 70 milliliters of glacial acetic acid to a 250 milliliter flask I then added in small portions 6.9 grams of ammonium bicarbonate the mixture bubbled and after it stopped bubbling I moved on I next added three point three grams of benzo benzaldehyde I attached a reflux condenser above the flask and heated the contents to boiling the mixture was boiled for a total of two hours after finishing reflux it was cooled to room temperature and poured into 200 milliliters of 5% ammonia solution tested the mixtures pH and found it to be only slightly acidic I moved on to filtering because of this the crude product was filtered off under vacuum washed with a small amount of water and transferred to a 150 milliliter beaker recrystallization I added in some 95 percent ethanol the beaker was covered with a watch glass and heated to boiling I continued adding in ethanol until almost everything dissolved which took the boat the whole beaker it was then taken off heating and cooled to room temperature it was then placed in a freezer overnight to cool it down to zero degrees Celsius pretty much everything had precipitated the pure product was filtered off under vacuum washed with a small amount of ethanol and dried thoroughly on the pump which removed nearly all of the ethanol nothing not everything was gone though so I added it to a watch glass placed it on my hot plate at low heat and dried to a constant mass I was left with one point six two grams of relatively pure loafing this represents a 58% yield this is somewhat low when it was more than I was expecting so I was pleased the show it's chemiluminescence I added 0.2 grams to a flask onto this solid I poured in 10 milliliters of methanol I first made solution B with one gram potassium hydroxide five milliliters methanol and 15 milliliters of water solution a was three milliliters of 3% hydrogen peroxide and topped up to 25 milliliters of water solution C was two milliliters of a percent sodium hypochlorite solution and topped up to 25 milliliters like the last one solutions a and B were poured into the flask with a low Fein solution see the light produced is somewhat weak and my camera is very bad at filming in the dark the human eye picks it up a lot better I put in two pictures of before and after adding the solution to show the light produced a little more clearly another demonstration uses air oxidation of loafing to produce chemiluminescence this produces a much longer glow and looks a lot cooler but unfortunately it is very very dim in my camera cannot pick it up at all I would like to extend a huge thank you to Aussie chemist and Piper Liam for their contributions to this channel through patreon I have linked their YouTube channels in the description of this video if you like what I do here and have a dollar or two to spare you can support Tom's lab on patreon calm all support is greatly appreciated thank you

title: Synthesis of Dibenzoylmethane
description:
Preparation of a 1,3-diketone by crossed Claisen condensation.

transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing diamonds oil methane by the claisen condensation diamonds oil methane apparently has some anti-cancer properties and it can also be used to make some heterocycles although I don't plan to use it for either of these purposes what I would like to do is make tribal luminescent crystals this is shown by nurdrage and I would like to do this if I ever get any europium first the ketone in this case acetophenone is deprotonated by a strong base this produces the carbon ion you see here which can also be written as the enol 8 the carbon ion is a good nucleophile and attacks the electrophilic carbonyl carbon of ethyl benzoate this pushes the pi electrons off onto the oxygen to give this intermediate the electrons then go back to form the double bond and push off the ethoxy leaving group this reforms the sodium ethoxide catalyst and produces the product but it isn't a very basic solution so one of the protons is pulled off the metal which then gives the enolate here which is rather stable and we finally have to do an acid workup to give the enol which is a top mer of the dive in soil ketone the enol form is actually more stable than the keto form because it forms this pseudo 6 membered ring by hydrogen bonding but for some reason we still call it by its keto form dive in soil methane first I added 20 milliliters of ethyl benzoate to a 50 milliliter round bottom flask next I added in three grams of acetophenone dissolved point eight five grams of sodium in about ten milliliters of dry ethanol the flask with the acetophenone and ethyl benzoate was set on the heating mantle and I set up this apparatus around it it consists of an addition funnel a condenser a clays an adapter and a long wire to let me disturb the mixture I added the sodium ethoxide solution in ethanol to the addition funnel when the flask had reached about 125 Celsius I started slow and careful addition of sodium ethoxide because the mixture was hotter than the boiling point of ethanol it started sputtering and I used the mixer to help this as soon as you add that oxide it begins to get very very thick this is why I had to use a metal wire because I did not have a mechanical stirrer and magnetic stirring will not work for something this thick eventually I had added all of the ethoxide solution and ethanol was boiled off with stirring this was continued until it appeared that no more ethanol was being driven off it left this very very viscous mixture you see here I let it cool down to room temperature and pour it in about ten milliliters of water to dissolve everything to my surprise it left a one-layer solution this was weird because I had water and ethyl benzoate and they should have separated but no matter because I added a solution of three grams sulfuric acid in ten milliliters of water and it separated into two layers this was separated washed with a sodium bicarbonate solution and then washed with water I set up for distillation and started by distilling off the ethyl acetate once all the ethyl acetate came off I applied vacuum and started distilling the mixture under vacuum at a vacuum of about 30 Torr ethyl benzoate came over at about 90 Celsius also unused acetophenone will come over as well the mixture boiled very violently and was very close to but did not splash over into the receiving vessel I ended up recovering about 90% of the unused ethyl benzoate eventually the flask reached about 150 Celsius and nothing else was coming over so I took it off heat and allowed it to cool and left these crystals here for some reason the footage just disappeared but i recrystallized the dirty Brown solid you see here in about 10 milliliters of methanol to give some lightly yellow pure crystals of diamonds oil acetone I got 1 point 6 3 grams or a percent yield of 30.3% a huge thank you goes out to the gayest person on patreon as well as Aussie chemist Clifford Robinson a wold and Piper Liam thank you guys so much for your contributions links a
safety: sodium hydride reacts violently with water

transcript: lab in this video I will prepare diphenyl methane by the reduction of benzophenone the reducing agent will be sodium in ethanol which reduces the aliphatic part of the molecule grams of benzophenone 1.5 grams of sodium metal and 20 milliliters of ethanol the set up II I'm using consists of a three neck round bottom flask with a Liebig condenser first I added the benzophenone which took a little bit of force because of the small neck this was followed by the ethanol next I turned on water so I condenser and heat to the heating mantle and I waited for the mixture to boil in the meantime I set up a carbon dioxide generator I added approximately equal volumes of sodium bicarbonate and sodium bisulfate by then the flask was boiling so I added the first small piece of sodium I waited for it to mostly react before adding the next one and repeated this until I was done done and had turned yellow over the course the reaction I turned off the heating mantle I started co2 generation by adding water to the generator and bubbled it through the flask for a few minutes the contents to a fifth about 50 milliliters of water when done some dense yellow blobs remained on the bottom and floated around in the solution because of the small volume the toluene extraction which followed was done in the beaker I used approximately 10 milliliters of toluene for each washing pipetted off times the resulting toluene solution was about 20 milliliters in volume and cloudy so I added some counsel chloride pellets to dry it it did its job very well so i decanted the toluene into a flask for distillation boiling ceased I then applied vacuum for a few minutes to remove the last bits of toluene still-warm solution into a clean bottle I know what you're saying Tom how do we know that this is diphenyl methane well the liquid had no odor of toluene and none of benzophenone which has a very distinctive odor and it's low melting point is what we expected it does not freeze probably because it is impure but unfortunately there's not enough to distill and recrystallization would lead to oiling out because of the low melting point

or Ben's own F Thanos and thulium bromide I was especially excited when I found out that it was relatively easy to make the reaction will take three steps the first step you react to naphthol with benzaldehyde in acetic acid to make a xanthine derivative then the next reaction you ranked the xanthine with bromine of acetic acid to make the xanthium bromide salt and finally you exposed a solution of the xanthine Liem salt in a sea of nitrile to ultraviolet light to create the final Phoenix product there's a lot to do so let's get right into it first I added 3.6 grams of 2-naphthol to a 25 milliliter round bottom flask this was followed by 10 milliliters of glacial acetic acid 0.5 milliliters of 6 molar hydrochloric acid and 1.3 5 grams of benzaldehyde stirring was started and the flask was heated with a hot water bath the 2-naphthol dissolved 100 Celsius for a few hours and the water bath wasn't getting quite hot enough so after everything dissolved I removed the water bath and put the flask directly over the hot plate after about two and a half hours I had to go to bed so I turned off heat and came back to the flask in the morning to find this crystals you see here to be sure that none of the solid was 2-naphthol i heated the flask again to 100 Celsius pretty much no solid dissolved so I let the flask cool back down to room temperature and filtered off the solid of course everything was washed with a small amount of acetic acid and dried thoroughly on the pump the product of this reaction was 3.06 grams of the xanthine derivative which corresponds to a yield of 68.5% now let's move on to reaction 2 I started by setting up an oil bath and getting it heated to get up to 100 degrees in the meantime I got the reactants ready 2.6 3 grams of the xanthine was added cool and then the crystals were filtered off finishing up this reaction left me with 0.2 four grams of the sparkling oranges and thulium bromide salt which is a percent yield of 31.1% I began the final reaction by adding 30 milliliters of Osito nitrile to 100 milligrams of the Santilli Umbro mind the write-up I was following specified for hours in full sunlight because I am in Maine and it is late fall I didn't really have this option I first tried this reaction with a handheld 395 nanometer flashlight which didn't work at all I then moved on to this UV water purifier which should be version of what I did so thank you very much and

transcript: lab it's been more time than I care to admit since the last video but I'm finally back in this video I'll be preparing some palladium on carbon a versatile hydrogenation catalyst palladium on carbon of the 5 and 10% variety are readily available online although they are relatively expensive a gram of 5% palladium on carbon is going to cost you somewhere in the range of 10 to 15 dollars that amount of palladium on carbon only contains a couple dollars of palladium metal so it can be quite a lot cheaper to make it yourself my starting point was palladium metal I bought a one gram and get for around $45 the first step in preparing palladium on carbon was dissolving the palladium to get it into a form that can be deposited on the carbon palladium will dissolve into nitric acid at room temperature although the reaction is very slow in order to dissolve any usable amount of palladium you'll have to heat the mixture after several hours it didn't look like that much a dissolved so I added in some hydrochloric acid in the hopes that it would speed it up eventually I got impatient so I poured off the solution and weighed the remaining piece of palladium to find out that I dissolved 0.6 grams of the metal which was enough for my purposes this palladium solution could probably be used for the reaction but I wasn't sure if the nitric acid would interfere and the preparation I was following called for palladium chloride so the next step was precipitating out the Palladium using a few grams of potassium hydroxide this gave me a nice chocolatey Brown precipitate which then had to be filtered off the problem was it was so fine that it immediately clogged my glass frit and it was filtering so slow that it would never have finished it turned out to not be a big deal though because I found that simply heating the mixture caused the particles to stick together and settle out rather quickly at this point I must have gotten distracted by another project because all the palladium ended up sitting on the bench for around 5 months I found all the palladium materials when I was cleaning up my lab and I decided to start working on it again using some six molar hydrochloric acid I washed all the palladium into one flask after sitting for a couple of hours everything resolved and I was left with a nice clear red palladium solution during that time I started preparing the carbon my carbon source is some activated carbon and I measured out about 10 grams maybe a little bit more I put the activated carbon in a beaker and covered it with some 10% nitric acid I then put this on my hot plate and let it heat up to around 60 or 70 Celsius and let it sit there for a few hours after heating I let it cool down and filtered off the carbon I also watched it several times with water until it was no longer acidic I then put the carbon on the side to dry and began preparing a formaldehyde solution my source of formaldehyde is para formaldehyde and the preparation I was looking at called for a 37% solution to prepare this I mixed together of water para formaldehyde and a tiny bit of potassium hydroxide and let it stir on my hot plate with some heating until everything dissolved after doing all these steps I had everything that I needed to make the Palladium on carbon I added my acid-washed carbon to a beaker and covered it in water I then got this heating on the hot plate and heated it up to 80 degrees Celsius at the same time i heated up the Palladium solution so the temperature would stay around ad C when I poured it in when everything was up to temperature I poured in the palladium chloride solution and this was immediately followed by the formaldehyde solution I then quickly used some 30% sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH to neutral after this I let the solution to sit and stir for around five minutes then I took it off the hot plate and let it cool down to room temperature the carbon started to settle out and you could see that the solution was no longer colo
safety: nitric acid is corrosive and oxidizing

transcript: lab in this video I will be making one three diaper a low propane this alkyl halide is a colorless liquid no surprise there with a surprisingly high density compared to other brominate organics like bromoform or tetra bubble methane two grams per milliliter isn't that high but it was surprising nonetheless I've had plans to make 1 3 dipolar propane for a very long time and in fact you can see it on my to-do list from over a year ago to make cyclopropane I know that I hardly ever get around to doing what I say but I did just buy this Dewar condenser so I am hopeful that I will get that done without further ado let's get on to the preparation the procedure actually comes from 1921 if you can believe it straight from the journal orcs in the chemists back then were absolute mad labs and even though I did this on a 10% scale I still needed a 1 liter flask to do it I started out with 135 milliliters of water 150 4.5 grams of sodium bromide forty five point six grams of one three propane dial and 250 grams of concentrated sulfuric acid first I added the water to a 1 liter flask along with all the sodium bromide of course it didn't all dissolve so I started adding the first half of the sulfuric acid that quickly turned out to be a bad idea so it showed the acid in the water before continuing after that I poured in the propane dial and the rest of the sulfuric acid for four and a half hours Morrison recommended five to six hours but this was late the night before Thanksgiving so I cut it short the crude product out of the reaction mixture [Music] our desired product the distillate separated into two layers the lower one being organic purification by washing the lower layer with water sulphuric acid which gave our second good sign the product was denser than the acid non-common feet carbonate solution and dried over calcium chloride for 24 hours interesting effect hydrated calcium chloride is less dense and the density of 1/3 diaper ammo propane is just right that the anhydrous and monohydrate particles sink while the dihydrate and higher float because some calcium chloride is sitting on the bottom we can be sure that this is dry I filtered off the dense liquid and set up for a final distillation because a column would be very slow with the high distillation temperature and I didn't have an accurate vacuum gauge for vacuum distillation one three diaper old propane boils at 167 degrees so I insulated everything well with aluminum foil there didn't appear to be anything boiling after that I collected everything between 166 and 168 degrees before the still had temperature started dropping everything was transferred to a tiered 100 milliliter graduated cylinder point two seven grams which is a percent yield of 81.2% not bad considering the small scale I found the density to be 1.96 five grams per milliliter which is very close to the literature value of one point nine eight nine grams per milliliter this should be pure enough for my purposes and we can confirm the identity in the next video when I make cyclopropane this video was made possible by the support of those on patreon their support is what allows me to buy things like this to continue to make videos with thank you to everyone that continued to support me even after my months of inactivity a special thanks goes out to my top patron Jay as well as the other incredible supporters Applied Science Ronan I'd the gayest person on YouTube Oliver and Maki finally thank you Andrew Aussie chemist cliffs music lab Daniel Josie and more let see you guys in the next one

title: Synthesis of Cyclopropane by the Wurtz Reaction
description:
In this video I prepare and condense cyclopropane, the smallest cycloalkane. The procedure was made up as I went along, roughly based on examples of the Wurtz reaction in Vogel's Textbook.

today I'm making something that I've wanted to make for a very long time cyclopropane is the smallest cycloalkane and it's rather unusual because of how strained it is most of its strain comes from angle strain and each carbon has four substituents and the farthest apart that four electron domains could be is 109.5 degrees so ideally the angle between the bonds would be 109.5 degrees but because it is three carbon atoms in a in an equilateral triangle the angle between the atoms has to be 60 degrees so that's very very far from ideal the carbon-carbon bonding orbitals aren't actually 60 degrees apart they are closer to the ideal angle 109.5 I think it's 104 degrees apart but that means that they can't overlap as much which makes a weaker bond and also because the the orbitals are farther out they're not not between the nuclei that means that the electron density is kind of following a curved path in the bonds and this is sometimes referred to as bent bonds which is very interesting cyclopropane was first prepared in 1881 by the verts reaction that's a carbon-carbon coupling reaction between two alkyl halides I put bromine here but that could be anything using metallic sodium and you can see if if these R's were connected to each other and the ends were connected then we could actually end up forming a ring and that's exactly what we do in the case of cyclopropane the reaction starts with sodium donating its electron the the bromine ends up breaking away and it leaves a single electron to create a radical on the terminal carbon then another sodium come along donate its electron and then the carbon can have a pair and that makes a carbon ion and as you can imagine this is a pretty good nucleophile and here is a partially positive carbon so that can attack there and it pushes off the bromine forming the desired product cyclopropane this isn't the only reaction happening obviously the other end of this carbon chain it could have the same thing so you could have a radical on both ends and those could join making cyclopropane and there are a number of side reactions that can happen to this radical can react with one on a different molecule and also this nucleophilic substitution this can happen on another one three die per mole of propane or a radical but luckily in this case the intramolecular reaction is more favorable because it doesn't require a collision for that to happen and also this carbon ion that could pull a hydrogen off of something and eliminate bromine and make some alkenes so it's a little bit messy and yields title: Test for carbonates and bicarbonates
description:
Sorry guys. Later i have found that CaCl2 does give ppt with both carbonate and bicarbonate. And for distinguishing its better to use magnesium sulphate. You should see appearance of some cloudiness when i added CaCl2 soln in the sodium bicarbonate soln. In theory we should get a clear soln but practically this happens. Magnesium sulphate gives clear solution with sodium bicarbonate

Carbonate reacts with magnesium ions to produce  magnesium carbonate which is a white ppt soluble in HCl
Bicarbonate reacts with calcium ions to form magnesium carbonate bicarbonate(soluble) which decomposes to magnesium carbonate on boiling thus forming a white ppt
In common both bicarbonate and carbonates react with HCl to give CO2 gas which evolves with effervescence,odourless and turns lime water milky

title: Test for Chloride ions(new)
description:
Preliminary test for Chloride ion- chloride reacts with conc H2SO4 to give HCl gas

1st Confirmatory test : a glass rod dipped in ammonia is brought near the mouth of the test tube- dense white fumes are evolved.

2nd confirmatory test : a pinch of MnO2 is added to the same experimental test tube- Chlorine gas evolved which bleaches moist litmus paper

3rd confirmatory test : take soda extract acidified with dil HNO3 and add AgNO3 soln → curdy white ppt which is soluble in NH4OH

4th confirmatory test : Chromyl Chloride test . 
Watch the video till end

title: Synthesis of Thymolphthalein
description:
in this video we will be making thymolphthalein  .it is an acid base indicator which is blue in alkaline medium . 
it is also used as disappearing ink

phthalic anhydride was prepared from phthalic acid
Thymol was purchased from a pansari shop . 
thymol is called "ajwain ka sat" ,it is available in indian market . It is an active ingredient of ayurvedic medicine "amrit dhara" 

thymol and phthalic anhydride are heated in a boiling water bath in presence of concentrated sulphuric acid. 
then the thymolpthalein formed is extracted with hexane .

title: Purification and Drying: Diethyl Ether
description:
Today we purify and dry the diethyl ether we preparde in a previous video. The procedure yields very dry ether which can therefore be used in water sensitive reactions, e.g. the grignard reaction.

I'm sorry this is only such a short video, but I've been kind of busy lately. Stay tuned though, because I have a lot cool ideas for the future.

As always: Enjoy!
